action_choice_hints: The candidate action cell is adjacent to a cell with a special directional marker.
The candidate action cell is not blocked by impassable elements in the immediate neighborhood.
The candidate action cell is positioned such that selecting it extends a contiguous path or structure.
The candidate action cell is aligned with the current position of a movable entity.
The candidate action cell is not already part of a previously marked or completed structure.
The candidate action cell is positioned to maintain connectivity between key objects or regions.
feature_family_hints: Adjacency relations: Needed to determine if a candidate cell is next to important objects or markers.
Path connectivity: Required to assess whether selecting a cell extends or preserves a continuous structure.
Occupancy status: Necessary to distinguish between empty, occupied, or already marked cells.
Alignment with movable entities: Needed to relate candidate actions to the positions of agents or targets.
Connectivity preservation: To ensure that actions do not disconnect important regions or objects.
causal_transition_hints: Choosing a cell adjacent to a directional marker tends to enable movement or extension in that direction.
Selecting a cell that is not blocked allows the creation or continuation of a path.
Choosing a cell already marked tends to avoid any state change or may be disallowed.
Selecting a cell that maintains connectivity prevents fragmentation of important structures.
Choosing a cell aligned with a movable entity tends to cause that entity to advance or interact with the environment.
